shirt.d: A piece of clothing that covers the upper body.
shirt.e: The boy was wearing a blue shirt to school.

studio.d: A room or building where artists work or perform.
studio.e: The musician was practicing her song in the recording studio.

suffering.d: Feeling very sad, hurt, or uncomfortable.
suffering.e: The puppy was suffering from a cold and needed help.

salt.d: a white substance used to flavor and preserve food
salt.e: I added a pinch of salt to the soup.

scottish.d: relating to the country of Scotland
scottish.e: The Scottish highlands are very beautiful.

severe.e: The storm caused severe damage to the town.

shift.e: The wind shifted and blew in a different direction.

stations.d: A place where things happen or where people work.
stations.e: The train station is where the train stops to pick up passengers.

saving.d: Keeping money safe so you can use it later.
saving.e: I'm saving my money to buy a new bike.

sees.d: To be able to look at and understand something.
sees.e: The student sees the answer written on the board.

settlement.d: An area where people have started to live and build homes.
settlement.e: The new settlement is growing quickly with more houses every year.

shots.d: Small injections of medicine into your body.
shots.e: I need to get my yearly flu shots soon.

sing.d: To make musical sounds with your voice.
sing.e: The choir will sing during the school concert.

somewhat.e: The weather is somewhat cold today.

sons.d: A male child of a parent
sons.e: My sons like to play outside together.

stood.d: To be in a upright position on your feet
stood.e: The teacher stood at the front of the class.

stronger.d: More powerful or capable than before
stronger.e: After training, the athlete became stronger.

structures.d: Things that are built with materials
structures.e: The new school has many large structures.

suicide.d: Ending your own life on purpose
suicide.e: Suicide is a very serious problem that should be prevented.

sleeping.d: When you rest and dream at night.
sleeping.e: I like to go to bed early so I can get a good sleeping.

smoking.d: The act of breathing in smoke, usually from a cigarette.
smoking.e: Smoking is not good for your health.

solo.d: When you do something by yourself, without any help.
solo.e: The singer performed a solo song during the concert.

statements.d: A sentence that gives information or an opinion.
statements.e: The teacher asked the student to make a statement about the book.

stephen.d: A name that belongs to a person.
stephen.e: My friend Stephen is coming over to play later.

stores.d: A place where you can buy things, like a grocery store or a toy store.
stores.e: We went to the stores to buy some new clothes.

suffered.d: When you feel pain or are hurt.
suffered.e: The player suffered an injury during the game.

surgery.d: When doctors help fix a problem in your body.
surgery.e: The doctor said I need to have surgery on my knee.

scheme.d: a plan or way of doing something
scheme.e: The teachers made a new scheme to help students learn better.

sight.e: My grandma's sight is not as good as it used to be.

sought.d: to try to find or get something
sought.e: The detective sought clues to solve the mystery.

sample.d: A small amount of something that shows what the whole is like
sample.e: The teacher gave us a sample of the new cookies to try.

seeking.d: Trying to find or get something
seeking.e: The explorer was seeking new lands to discover.

significantly.d: In an important or noticeable way
significantly.e: The athlete improved significantly over the season.

situations.d: Circumstances or conditions that someone is in
situations.e: The students discussed different real-life situations.

storage.d: A place to keep things safe and organized
storage.e: We keep our extra supplies in the storage closet.

sufficient.d: Enough of something to meet a need
sufficient.e: I have sufficient money to buy the new toy.

suggests.d: To give an idea or recommendation about something
suggests.e: The recipe book suggests adding more spices.

scientists.d: people who study the natural world
scientists.e: Scientists work hard to understand how things work.

seats.e: The theater had many comfortable seats.

soil.d: the top layer of the ground where plants grow
soil.e: The farmer added fresh soil to the garden.

staying.e: I will be staying at home this weekend.

surely.e: I will surely remember your birthday.

switch.d: a button or lever that turns something on or off
switch.e: She flipped the light switch to make the room brighter.

shame.d: When you feel bad about something you did
shame.e: She felt shame after spilling her juice.

shares.d: When you give part of something to others
shares.e: The class shares the toys during playtime.

shipping.d: Sending things from one place to another
shipping.e: The package was ready for shipping.

smell.d: The way something feels or tastes to your nose
smell.e: The cookies had a delicious smell.

spoken.d: When you say words out loud
spoken.e: The teacher spoke to the class.

stayed.d: When you remain in one place
stayed.e: The family stayed at the hotel all weekend.

strongly.d: With a lot of force or power
strongly.e: The wind was blowing strongly outside.

surrounding.d: Things that are all around something else
surrounding.e: The park had trees surrounding it.

simon.d: A person's name, like the name of a boy or a girl
simon.e: My friend Simon likes to play outside.

stable.d: Something that doesn't move around a lot or change
stable.e: The horses live in a nice, stable barn.

studied.d: When you work hard to learn or practice something
studied.e: The student studied hard for the spelling test.

symptoms.d: Things your body does when you are sick
symptoms.e: The doctor asked about my fever symptoms.

santa.d: A kind, old man who brings presents at Christmas
santa.e: Santa comes down the chimney on Christmas Eve to deliver gifts.

scenes.d: Pictures or moments in a movie, show, or play
scenes.e: The movie had many exciting scenes that kept me watching.

shortly.d: In a short time, very soon
shortly.e: I will be home from school shortly, so please wait for me.

solar.d: Relating to the sun and the energy it provides
solar.e: The solar panels on the roof collect energy from the sun.

subsequent.d: Happening or coming after something else
subsequent.e: After the storm, the sunny weather was a subsequent event.

survive.d: To continue to live or exist, despite difficulties
survive.e: The plants in the garden survived the cold winter weather.

swear.d: To promise or declare something is true
swear.e: I swear I did not take the last cookie from the jar.

syria.d: A country in the Middle East
syria.e: Many people have had to leave their homes in Syria.

select.e: I need to select my favorite book to read.

sin.e: Lying is considered a sin in many religions.

solutions.e: The teacher gave us solutions to the math problems.

somehow.d: In some way that is not known
somehow.e: I somehow lost my favorite toy.

sydney.e: My aunt lives in the city of Sydney.

semi.d: A large truck that pulls a large trailer.
semi.e: The semi-truck carried boxes of toys to the toy store.

sharp.d: Having a very pointed or cutting edge.
sharp.e: The chef used a sharp knife to cut the vegetables.

shoulder.d: The top part of your arm where it connects to your body.
shoulder.e: The boy hurt his shoulder when he fell off his bike.

silence.d: When there is no sound or noise.
silence.e: The class sat in silence while the teacher spoke.

singer.d: A person who sings, especially as their job.
singer.e: The famous singer performed on stage for the crowd.

soccer.d: A game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's goal.
soccer.e: The kids played a game of soccer in the park.

soldier.d: A person who is part of an army and fights in wars.
soldier.e: The soldier marched with his fellow soldiers to defend their country.

spiritual.d: Related to the soul or spirit, not just the physical body.
spiritual.e: The spiritual leader spoke about finding peace within.

supplies.d: The things you need to do a certain job or activity.
supplies.e: The teacher gave the students the supplies they needed for the art project.

suspect.d: Someone who is thought to have done something wrong.
suspect.e: The police questioned the suspect about the missing money.
